sentences.text: Hello!
Good afternoon, everyone!
Good afternoon, how are you?
I'm doing pretty well.
… Lots going on.
Happy midweek, Lance!
We are….
Thank you.
the threshold, we are past midweek.
So, this….
Other side of it.
Oh, interesting.
No cat filter today?
No, I know, it's so weird, I don't know what it's gonna be on.
Well, this is Zoom, so on Teams it's typically there.
I liked it.
I did too.
Make a trend where we add fun things to our backgrounds.
Do you have cats, Anne-Marie?
You have to, right?
No, I… I mean, I did many, many years ago, but no, it's just a… it's just a kind of, like, a cat that lands on my head when I'm in teams, and I haven't been able to figure out how to get it off.
It's like an animated cat, you know.
Yeah, we saw!
So, love.
It's cool.
I don't know, maybe, like, psychologically, Teams is trying to tell me that I need a little bit more love in my life, I don't know.
That's great.
Well, my daughters really want a cat right now, so I… I don't know, we'll see.
A ragdoll cat, I don't know if you've ever heard of those.
No.
They're like.
It's a.
Aren't they the grumpy-looking ones?
I don't know, they're supposed to be… they're the most like a dog.
of the cats, which I'm like, then why don't we get a dog?
Yeah.
Get a dog.
I have a dog, and I absolutely adore her.
There's nothing to me better than the companionship of a dog.
They're just a lot more responsibility.
True.
What kind of dog do you have?
She's a half Shih Tzu and half… Bichon, so she looks like a… kind of like a Maltese.
She's a little white.
She said that.
Horrible.
Thank you for that, Lance.
Now we know what to talk about.
Exactly right.
Oh, I thought they were, like, grumpy cats.
I must be thinking of the wrong kind.
Apparently, these cats, apparently, they're, like, $3,000, and they're… they live 20 years.
Wow.
And they're supposed to be amazing, but… I don't know, landscape….
I got my dog for, like, $700, so I feel like it's a budget-friendly option.
And, I mean, look at him now.
Aww.
Ted's not even on his bed, he's just living the dream.
You can get cats for, like, free, basically.
Everywhere you go, you can adopt a cat.
Yeah, Dol Saba….
Turn the corner, you got one, Patrick.
Yep.
I say, seriously, I… but why do I need to pay 3 grand for a cat when I just get one for 20 bucks?
But… we'll see.
Yeah.
Especially in Wisconsin, I feel like there's… there's barn cats everywhere.
Oh, yeah.
Ask a farm for one.
Annemarie, where are you based again?
Are you in Florida?
No, I'm in Dallas-Fort Worth area.
You are in Dallas, okay.
Yes.
Nice.
Nice.
Lance, you're in Florida.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yes, I am.
Nice.
Cool.
Cool.
Well, thank you guys for hopping on, and making the time to chat about this.
So, as we get kickoff started, we like to kind of, like, over-communicate where we're at with implementation.
get on the same page with what else is needed, not only for implementation, but for a successful rollout of this.
So, we put a little deck together to go over Just what the partnership will entail, and make sure that we have everything that we need.
So… To get started, we're thinking… agenda for the day, this looks more daunting than I think it will feel, but, meet the Maven team.
So you've met Patrick and myself, but we'll introduce Paige, who will also be working with the Highmark team.
Just aligned around desired outcomes, so how you're measuring success of this, what a success will look like for these 6 properties.
We know we're kind of starting this as a pilot for the rest of the portfolio, so I want to make sure we really nail that down.
Like I just said, go over implementation, know that we got that document from you, Lance, this morning, so it looks like everything's been sent on your end, but we'll go over that.
And then, what to expect post-kickoff.
So, once these go live, what will the partnership look like?
And then we do have a document in here for best practices.
We'll send this deck over so you have that document.
And then any next steps on our end, or questions that you all have.
Anything else you guys wanted to add into that?
Not me.
Alright.
Lance did all the heavy lifting on our side, I really haven't had to do anything.
Yeah, yes, I saw that.
Definitely easy to have all of the links in one place, so I appreciate you doing that.
Well, you guys have met me, been with the company, like, 7 years, I'm on the sales side, working with all of our largest customers across the country.
And then Paige, I'll let you introduce yourself.
Okay, hello, I'm Paige.

I am your Customer Success Manager, so I will be working hand-in-hand with Kylie and Patrick throughout the pilot and the entire relationship, just making sure, you know, product is working as it should, getting your feedback, we're meeting your expectations that everything goes smoothly.
And the creature in his bed is named Boone.
for me.
He'll be here, too.
Boone will make his appearances.
Yeah, he's an emotional support dog.
Patrick, anything else you want to add?
I just, just try not to be offended I didn't make the slide here, but… I know.
Sorry.
We met on the first Zoom, so I lead the sales team, I work with Kylie and Paige.
Closely, and we're excited.
Lance, I see you on the Zoom, we've met many times, and I'm excited to be working with you, you and your team, so….
Absolutely.
And, get this off on the right foot.

Jumping into the contract overview, nothing earth-shattering on this one, you just signed the agreement, but just so we are on the same page with what we have signed up, today, is the Google Business AI tool for the 6 properties.

So, that includes everything that we went over for the Google tool, the daily posts, the products with amenities feed, concessions, Q&A, We did also talk about the social AI tool, but the consensus was to hold off on that for now.
So the agreement for this, we're getting it started now, we have implementation started, your… the term date is invoicing start of 9-1, through the following year.
Any questions on that?
Annemarie, I'm curious, I think you might have put it in the email, but for these 6 properties, is it a specific client that, was for these 6, or how'd we land on these?
Yeah, no, that's a good question.
It's not a specific client, it's a specific regional manager.
Okay.
They're properties in a market that I am not as familiar with, being Michigan and Indiana.
They're a little bit smaller, and they're… they don't have the kind of… well, they don't have the kind of competition that we have in Dallas.
The… Their market seems to be pretty, … I don't think they shop around quite as much as we're accustomed to.
So, I don't really spend a lot of marketing on them.
They seem to do okay, and at the same time, this is the one regional manager that I have more of a difficult time convincing to spend money.
And so she's the only 6 properties I have that haven't been on a form of this kind of product before, despite the success that we have had.
So, it just seemed like a good opportunity to… to try this with her.
She was open to it.
And, … You know, and then from there, we'll just… we'll just see.
So that… I kind of hope that answers your question, but she's like, the last 6 properties that really wasn't on board with doing anything with Google Business Profile.
I'm like, we need to try this, because you haven't made the leap yet, so let's make the leap here.
No, that helps.
I have two follow-ups.
So, how many regional managers do you support, at… Six.
Okay, 6.
And then where in Indiana are these properties?
In Michigan?
like, Indianapolis, pretty much, in that area.
There's… there's two in Indianapolis, and then there's 4 in Michigan, which is, like, Lansing, and… Saginaw, I think it's Saginaw.
They kind of go the loop.
They're… they're… it would… they're not that close together.
Okay Well, we can… I mean, as we talk and onboard these and have these properties live, one of our biggest customers is based in Indiana and Michigan, that's Edward Rose & Sons.

So they have 150 properties on this tool, and in markets that you just mentioned, so you can kind of share some of the best practices from them with you as we, you know, as you communicate with that regional, too.
So….
That's it.
Cool.
Cool.
Well, just jumping into a little bit of what we heard on the last call.
So, reasons that you guys had decided to move forward with this.

Wanted to put this in here as just a little bit of a recap, but that there was a need to expand features from the current Google Business process, and that's why you guys took the call in the first place.
And that you had some properties that were doing some manual posting and manual uploading to the Google profiles.

Obviously, we know that that process is time-consuming, it takes a lot of time and effort to not only create the content, but then also schedule the content out, and that automation would be helpful for, efficiency for the team, and then consistency across profiles.

And then the… the features that caught your attention the most were the pricing and availability feed in the products feed, with that real-time floor plan level pricing, the concessions, so pulling concessions directly from the feed to the profiles, and then automating Q&A.
Is there anything that I'm missing there?
Nope, not for me.
Alright.
Jumping into onboarding timeline.
So, obviously the call today is the first step, getting on the kickoff call and meeting with us, aligning on goals and timeline of not only implementation, but the rollout.
… Next steps would be implementation itself.
So, Lance, it looks like from that document you sent over, you have activated all of the feeds and given access to Google for all 6 of these?
Yes, you'll probably end up seeing those feeds go in tomorrow, because I finished them off this morning.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah, I checked with our implementation team right before the call, they hadn't seen them yet, but it does usually… it can take up to 24 hours.
So, if we don't see them tonight, we'll see them come in tomorrow.
Those are really the only two steps that we need on your end to get this live.
So, a little bit of that waiting time, and then as soon as we get those feeds, our team will build out the features on the profile.
And then they'll go live.
So, I would expect this to be wrapped up in the next few days.
I'll keep you in the loop along the way if it happens to be sooner than that.
But either way, we'll send an email out when these are live, so you can review them before they go out.
Next steps after this, so after these 6 properties are live, we do like to do ongoing check-ins.
So, we do recommend in the beginning, as we're getting this kicked off, that we meet about every month.

Obviously, we'll work around your timeline and what works best for you, but that's our recommendation as we get this rolled out, we have new features, we want to make sure these profiles are fully optimized as early as possible.
Does that timeline work for you guys, monthly?
It works for me.
Okay.

I think one thing you'll notice as you work with us is this project is built in-house, and a lot of the features and enhancements are just feedback and requests from our customers, so encourage you both as you as we roll this out, and what you see, if you think of anything that you're like, hey, I think this would be cool if we build this, or this would be a little better if this was tweaked, let us know, because that drives the product.
So….
Great.
Some of your feedback as you get going.
So then in terms of implementation, don't need to spend too much time on this, because we do have the first two steps done already.
The last step, after these go live, there are some ways that we can customize, at a property level.
I think we talked about this a little bit on the demo, but we can customize, like, your brand voice at a property level, as well as insert SEO keywords throughout the posts.

So, if that's something that you guys want to do to customize a little bit more, we do have a document that I can send over, to get these properties really sounding and feeling like your property, and then get those SEO keywords to help your organic traffic.
Yeah, absolutely.
So here's an example of what this looks like.
And if you guys have a process in place already, or brand guidelines put together, you can just send us those if that's easier.
Otherwise, the doc is language preferences, so if you like to say units versus apartments versus apartment homes, you can label that here.
If there's any terms that you want to exclude at the property level, we can do that as well.
And then the SEO keywords.
So, some examples here of what other customers have provided.
But if, again, if you have this in a different format, you can send it that way, too.
I don't want to create more work for you.
For the SEO keywords, are we able to use, like, tokens, apartments, and city, apartments and zip code?
Yep.
Okay.
Emery, we'll go over that.
I know I saw a question come in, but I haven't been able to dive into it, and I'll explain that, but we can actually pull some of that, those main keywords as well.
Okay.
Great.
Yep.
Yeah, send us those, however's easiest.
If it's in this doc, if it's in another file you already have going.

And then if you have anything, any information seasonality-wise, like, obviously in the Midwest, we'll want to not be advertising pools in February, and we already know that on our end, but if there's anything else unique.
You can let us know that here.
We'll get this inserted on the back end to make sure we're… we're not advertising things that are closed.
Okay.
And then jumping into post-kickoff.
So, we already talked a little bit about the ongoing check-in calls.
We'll get those on the calendar, every month.
At least in the beginning, we'll start doing those.
And then I have… I'll send this deck over, but we have a support email, and then a billing email as well.
Paige and I, if you want to just email us, we'll get it to the right place as well.
But just so you have all of the resources in one place, wanted to get these on the deck.
Any questions on that?
Should be pretty quick and painless for y'all, especially if Lance is over there compiling everything we need.
Yeah.
He's….
Good.
On top of everything.

There's a… I've done so many of these, and I realized that it… because this is going to be close to, you know, the Zumper thing that we have, and I figured if we do end up having additional properties, we can use this.

I almost actually pulled in everything that you guys had, and I was gonna ask if it's easier, I don't know if you guys use a form, … for when you submit it to get, like, the address and everything, the invoice number.
Realistically, we could… we could use this, for future, but that's something we can address then.
Yeah, we have a few different ways that we've done it with other customers.

We have some that like to use this sort of a sheet, and then they'll add the properties and then send us an email that they added a property or two, and then we'll get the property-level contracts out.
We do have another customer that signed an MSA, and then they use a Smartsheet.
I don't know if you have any familiarity with Smartsheet.
It's similar to this, but it's a software that, as soon as you enter something, we get notified, and we have it set up so that Paige and I get notified, and then our implementation team does as well.
So.
they're adding properties pretty rapidly, so it made more sense there.
But we could continue using this, or look at other options.
Yeah, absolutely, whatever's easiest.
I figured, this is gonna be the cleanest way for us to start.
Yeah, yeah, we'll keep this going, and then if we do want to talk about other options, we can do that too.
The last thing I have in here is some best practices.
So, we put this together for, more of, like, a resource, so you can see the, like, quality of photos that appeared best on Google, and just some recommendations.
So, I'll keep this in the DAC and send this over so you have it as well.
Kylie, can you, … Remind me how you guys pull the photos.
Are you… are you scrubbing them, or are we providing you the most high-quality versions?
We are pulling what's in the feed.
So, the version of photos that you have coming through your property management software feed, that's where we're getting them.
So, like, your marketing.
Gotcha, okay.
Just making sure, okay.
And these, these properties….
I believe there's, like, 2 of them that have a model, there's a couple that don't.
And I'm not thrilled with a lot of the pictures that they have.
What's kind of interesting about these properties, too, is that they… they lack amenities.
That's what kind of makes it challenging.
I mean, one of the properties has some decent amenities, I think it's, like, Woodland Lakes.
has, you know, a playground, and I think they've got a, a tennis court or something like that.
But a lot of them don't have… Like, wow amenities.
You know, it's like the smaller kind of gym.
they may have a pool, but like you said, it's not open all the time, because they're gonna start to get cold weather, you know, in another week or so.
So….
To me, that's just always been the challenge, posting there.

And I just as an FYI, because some of them, when I look at their photos online, so now they're, with the regional manager, which, I mean, I… she's great, I love her, so I'm not complaining, but she just… her view of things a little bit differently, so in terms of, like, just getting out there and doing photo shoots and getting the best visuals of things, where I feel like this region lacks compared to some other regions in that regard.
Yep.

Having said that, I think it's gonna be really helpful to have these… this feedback, not just from us to you, but from you to us, because… … it gives me an opportunity to go back and say, you know, we really do need to get this particular photo shoot done right now, because it's just going to shine better in the advertising.
Because you're relying on, you know, Apartments.
com, which is going to take the photos for them, and put it on their advertising, and of course, that's not going to reflect back onto our website.
So, if you see things that… I guess, I guess, you know, all that to say, if you see things and you're like, you know what, this isn't the best.
Just… let's keep this that open, because then I have more ammunition to go back and say, you know, we need to do this now.
Yep.
Yeah.
Yeah, and that's another one of the reasons for the check-in calls, too.
So we can review properties that are live, we'll take screenshots directly from your profiles, and we can chat through things.
Obviously, the content… the more photos we have, the better, the more amenities we have, the better.
Obviously, that's how we're creating the posts and the photos.
But we'll work with what we can, and if we see an opportunity to get a couple more photos of a certain amenity, we'll do that, but we can chat through those and check.
Okay.
Yep.
Paige, Patrick, anything I'm missing?
No, that's good feedback, Anna Marie.
Like, that's something we'll have to make a note of and keep talking about.
So, why we put this best practice slide in front of folks, you know?
I think the main takeaway is everything is powered by that syndication feed, so if it's in your syndication feed, we'll pull it, like, the concession accuracy.
… And we just talked through the photos, but that's the source of truth for everything that we're doing.
Yeah, and I'll double-check with the regional manager to see,
On these properties, if there is anything that is specifically outdated.
Okay.
And, I'll get that information ASAP, and those are the ones that I'll push to get photos on.
I don't think, you know, I don't want to give you the impression that this is… it's bad, like, you're going in and they're, oh, they look, but they don't.

these properties look fine, it's just that from where I sit, looking at some of the properties I have in Dallas-Fort Worth, there's just more photos, and more amenities, and more of a selection of things to choose from, that's all.
Yeah.
Make sure that she is absolutely up-to-date on everything.
It's a best practice, like, it absolutely doesn't… it does not mean this product won't work, like.
Right.
purposes, it's always the text that matters the most, but we do recommend, hey, if you have a lot of photos.
That's usually, like, the best for us, but if you.
Yeah.
15, that's… that's fine, too.
I mean, we're starting off at… yeah, I mean, and to that point, Google Business Profile is performing already at all of these properties.
So, when I look into Google Analytics, and I look at where primarily, you know, most of their traffic is coming from.
it is Google Business Profile, so….
Nice.
People are going there.
So, you know, from there, we can only do better.
And then from that benchmark, you know, that… it just gives us an opportunity to kind of tweak and fine-tune, and then, you know, get more what you need to make it even better, so….
Yeah, perfect.
Yeah, well, we're excited to get this started and help out with that.
Me too.
Cool.
Well, I will send a follow-up email.
I'll put this deck in here so that you have, these sheets, this best practices.
I'll send over the brand guidelines as well.
Again, if there's an easier way for you guys to get us those SEO keywords, send it however.
And then I will send over some dates for the next check-in, and then we'll just keep this going on a monthly cadence.
When do you, … when is our estimated live date, then?
Should be the next couple days, so if we.
Okay, perfect.
that come through tomorrow, usually we can get it set up in about 2 days, so….
Okay.
Definitely before the weekend.
I'll check with implementation again in the morning and let you know if we're missing anything.
Okay, terrific.
Cool.
Anything else from you guys?
Oh, I'm all set, thank you.
All right.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Thanks, everybody.
Thank you, have a good day.
See ya.
Bye.
